skill.name: error-detective
skill.description: Search logs and codebases for error patterns, stack traces, and anomalies. Correlates errors across systems and identifies root causes. Use PROACTIVELY when debugging issues, analyzing logs, or investigating production errors.
skill.summary: Analyze logs, stack traces, and error patterns to identify likely root causes.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static findings were reviewed against SKILL.md and no confirmed security issue was found. The command execution finding is Markdown inline code for a resource path, and the weak cryptography finding is ordinary frontmatter text.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Resource Path Flagged as Command Execution
low_findings.description: SKILL.md line 26 references `resources/implementation-playbook.md` as Markdown inline code. It does not execute a shell command or request command execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a natural language instruction to open a documentation resource. No shell syntax, arguments, or executable command is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern in Frontmatter Text
low_findings.description: SKILL.md line 3 is part of the description field. It contains no cryptographic algorithm, key handling, hashing, encryption, or security-sensitive implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged line only describes searching logs and codebases. I found no evidence of DES, MD5, SHA1, or any cryptographic use.

content.value_statement: Production errors are hard to diagnose when logs, stack traces, and deployments are disconnected. This skill guides structured investigation, pattern extraction, and root cause hypothesis building.
actual_capabilities: Identify recurring error patterns across logs and stack traces.
Correlate error spikes with deployments, changes, or service failures.
Suggest regex patterns for extracting relevant error details.
Build timelines of error occurrences and related symptoms.
Produce root cause hypotheses supported by observed evidence.
Recommend monitoring queries to detect future recurrence.
limitations: Requires access to relevant logs, stack traces, or error samples.
Does not replace live observability tools or production telemetry.
Cannot confirm root cause without enough system context.
May need human validation before production fixes are applied.

use_cases.title: Investigate Production Incidents
use_cases.description: Analyze error spikes, stack traces, and recent changes to find likely incident causes.

use_cases.title: Debug Application Failures
use_cases.description: Turn noisy logs into grouped symptoms, candidate code locations, and next debugging steps.

use_cases.title: Improve Monitoring Coverage
use_cases.description: Convert recurring failure patterns into alerts, queries, and prevention checks.

prompt_templates.title: Summarize Error Logs
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these error logs. Group similar errors, identify repeated messages, and summarize the most important symptoms.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze a Stack Trace
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this stack trace. Identify the likely failing component, relevant code path, and the next checks I should run.

prompt_templates.title: Correlate Incident Signals
prompt_templates.prompt: Use these logs, timestamps, deploy notes, and service names to build an error timeline and identify likely correlations.

prompt_templates.title: Design Recurrence Detection
prompt_templates.prompt: From this incident evidence, propose monitoring queries, alert conditions, and prevention steps for detecting recurrence early.

output_examples.input: A batch of API logs showing intermittent payment failures after a deploy.
output_examples.output: The skill groups timeout errors, builds a timeline around the deploy, and suggests checking the payment client configuration.

output_examples.input: A Java stack trace and related service logs from a failed background job.
output_examples.output: The skill identifies the failing method, separates primary errors from follow-up failures, and lists targeted verification steps.

output_examples.input: Several days of search error messages with changing rates by region.
output_examples.output: The skill highlights regional clustering, suggests anomaly checks, and recommends alert queries for future detection.

best_practices: Provide timestamps, service names, deploy history, and representative error samples.
Separate raw evidence from assumptions when describing the incident.
Validate root cause hypotheses with targeted checks before applying fixes.
anti_patterns: Do not provide only one log line when the issue appears distributed.
Do not treat correlation with a deploy as proof of causation.
Do not ignore secondary errors caused by retries or cascading failures.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide logs, stack traces, timestamps, service names, deploy notes, and any known recent changes.

faq.question: Can it analyze logs from any platform?
faq.answer: Yes, it can reason over pasted logs from common platforms when the format and context are clear.

faq.question: Does it run log queries directly?
faq.answer: No. It can suggest query patterns, but users must run them in their own observability tools.

faq.question: Can it find the exact root cause?
faq.answer: It can identify likely causes with evidence, but final confirmation depends on system context and verification.

faq.question: Is it useful after an incident is resolved?
faq.answer: Yes. It can help summarize findings, improve alerts, and define prevention steps.

faq.question: Can it handle sensitive production logs?
faq.answer: Use redacted logs when possible. Remove secrets, tokens, customer data, and private identifiers before sharing.
